title: tina antongiliensis
side_effects: Tina antongiliensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach cramps due to its high alkaloid content. Prolonged consumption of the plant's extracts can also lead to liver and kidney damage, as well as increased heart rate and blood pressure. In rare cases, improper use of Tina antongiliensis may cause respiratory problems, including shortness of breath and difficulty swallowing. Additionally, excessive use of the plant may interact with other medications, causing adverse reactions and complications.
